originalTitle: Millions listen to Ethiopian star's song taking swipe at government
title: A Big Song from Ethiopia: Let's All Be Friends!
content:
Hi, everyone! Your super-duper news reporter for Africa is here! Guess what? There's a really special song making lots of noise in Ethiopia. It's from a very famous singer named Teddy Afro. He is like a music hero for many people! His new song is all about something very important. It's about being friends and working together. He wants everyone in his country to feel happy and strong, like a big family.

Who is Teddy Afro?

Teddy Afro is a superstar musician from Ethiopia. Imagine your favourite singer, but even more famous! He sings beautiful songs that many, many people love to listen to. When Teddy Afro sings, people all over Ethiopia listen. His music makes people happy, makes them dance, and sometimes makes them think about important things. He has a very special voice. **Did you know?** Ethiopia is a beautiful country in Africa. It is known for its really old history and yummy coffee!

What is a Song?

A song is like a story told with music! It has words that we call lyrics, and a tune that makes you want to tap your feet or sway. Songs can make us feel happy, sad, or even brave. Teddy Afro's new song has a very important message hidden in its music. He uses his voice to share a special wish with everyone. He wants everyone to hear what he has to say.

The Big Idea in His Song: Unity!

So, what is this big idea? It's called "unity." Unity means everyone working together and being friends. It means helping each other and caring for everyone, even if they are a little different from you. Imagine your school. If all the children play nicely together and help their teachers, that's unity! If everyone is on the same team, that's unity. It makes everything better and more fun. Teddy Afro's song is like a big hug for his country. He wants everyone to feel like they belong together. He sings about how wonderful it would be if everyone in Ethiopia felt like one big, happy family. **Did you know?** The word "unity" comes from "one." It means being one group, one team, or one family, even if there are many people!

Why is Unity Important?

Unity is super important because when people work together, amazing things can happen! Think about building a sandcastle. If all your friends help, the sandcastle will be bigger and stronger! If everyone in a country works together, they can make their home a wonderful place. They can build better schools, grow more food, and help sick people. Everyone benefits when there is unity. When people are united, they feel safe and happy. There is less fighting and more smiling. Teddy Afro sings about this happy feeling. He wants everyone to remember how strong they can be when they stand together.

What Does He Say About Our Country?

In his song, Teddy Afro gently says that sometimes, people in his country might forget to work together. He sings about how we can all try harder to be kind and help each other. He's not angry, he's just asking everyone to think about how they can make their country even better. He believes in the good hearts of people. He knows that everyone wants to be happy. His song is a reminder to always choose kindness and working together. He wants people to remember that even if they have different ideas, they can still be good friends and neighbours. He wants everyone to feel proud of Ethiopia and proud of each other. It's a song of hope and a wish for more happiness for everyone. **Did you know?** Ethiopia has many different groups of people who speak different languages. But they all share the same love for their country!

Music Helps Us Understand

Music is a very special magic! It can make us feel things in our hearts. When Teddy Afro sings about unity, his music helps people understand why it's so important. It helps them feel connected to each other. It's like his song is whispering, "Let's be friends, let's work together!" He uses his amazing talent to share this message with everyone. Music is a powerful way to share feelings and ideas. When millions of people listen, they all hear the same special words. This makes them think about how they can be more united in their own lives, at home, at school, and in their towns. It makes them want to make a difference. **Did you know?** Music has been part of human life for thousands of years! People sing and dance all over the world to celebrate and share messages.

Let's Listen Together!

Imagine all the children in Africa holding hands. That's a beautiful picture of unity! Teddy Afro's song reminds us that even little acts of kindness can make a big difference. Sharing your toys, helping a friend with homework, or saying "please" and "thank you" – these are all ways we can show unity. His song is like a gentle reminder that we are all stronger when we are together. So, when you hear a song, think about its message. Does it make you want to be a better friend? Does it make you want to help your family and your community? **Did you know?** The Ethiopian flag has a star in the middle, which often stands for hope and unity for all its people, shining bright!

A Message of Hope

So, there you have it! Teddy Afro, the amazing musician from Ethiopia, has sung a very special song. It's a song about unity, about being friends, and working together to make our country and our continent a happy place. His music makes people think, and it makes people want to be good to each other. Let's all listen to his message and try to be united every day! We can all make a difference, just like Teddy Afro's song.
summary: Ethiopian music star Teddy Afro has released a new song that encourages everyone in his country to be united and work together like friends, reminding them about the power of togetherness.
whyMatters: This is important because when people listen to special songs like this, it helps them remember to be kind, work together, and make their country a happy and strong place for everyone. Music can inspire us to be better friends and neighbours.

title: DR Congo accepts first set of deportees from the US
content: The Congolese government stresses those expelled from the US are only in the country temporarily.

originalTitle: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home
title: Zimbabwe's Special Stone Birds Are All Home!
content:
A Big Happy Day for Zimbabwe!

Hello, everyone! I have some super happy news from our friend, Zimbabwe, a beautiful country in sunny Africa! Imagine your very favorite toy. Now, imagine that toy was taken away from you a long, long time ago. And now, after many, many years, it's finally back home with you! That's how people in Zimbabwe are feeling right now. They are celebrating a very special homecoming! All of their famous stone birds are finally back in Zimbabwe. Every single one! This is a really big deal because these birds are not just toys, they are super important parts of their country's history and heart. The whole country is smiling from ear to ear!

Meet the Amazing Stone Birds!

These aren't just any birds. They are beautiful statues made of strong, heavy stone. They look like real birds, but they are carved out of rock, not alive! Some look like powerful eagles, with their chests puffed out, looking up at the sky. Others might look like elegant hawks, ready to fly. These birds are very, very old. They were made by super clever artists many, many years ago – before your great-grandparents were even born! Imagine someone carefully chipping away at a big rock to make something so detailed and special. They are not small birds either; some are quite big and heavy! Did you know? These special birds are sometimes called "Zimbabwe Birds"! They are so important that a picture of one is even on Zimbabwe's national flag! How cool is that?

A Very Old Place: Great Zimbabwe

Where did these amazing birds come from? They came from a very, very old city called Great Zimbabwe. This isn't a city with cars and tall glass buildings like we see today. It was a city with big, strong stone walls that were built without any cement! Long, long ago, smart and powerful people lived there. They were kings and queens and wise leaders who built this incredible place. The stone birds stood tall on top of the walls and pillars in this ancient city. They watched over the land and the people, like silent guardians. They were like storytellers, too, telling tales of the past without saying a word. They were very important to the leaders who lived there. They showed how strong and wise the people of Great Zimbabwe were. Did you know? The word "Zimbabwe" actually means "great stone houses" or "venerated houses" in the Shona language! The country is named after this incredible ancient place and its stone structures!

The Birds Went on a Long, Sad Journey

But then, something sad happened. Many, many years ago, people came from far, far away lands to Africa. These people were sometimes called "colonialists." They took over many places and sometimes took special and important things from these lands. They saw the beautiful stone birds at Great Zimbabwe and thought they were very interesting. So, they took the birds from their home. It was like someone taking your most cherished toy or your family's favorite storybook without asking, and carrying it far, far away across the big ocean. The birds were taken to different countries, to museums, and even to private homes of people who collected special things. Zimbabwe missed its birds very much. It was like a piece of their heart was missing, a piece of their history taken away.

Missing Our Friends

For many, many years, the people of Zimbabwe only saw pictures of their special birds. They knew these birds were a big part of their history and who they are as a country. The birds helped tell the story of their ancestors, the clever and powerful people who lived in Great Zimbabwe. When a country misses its special things, it's like missing a part of itself. It feels incomplete. The people hoped and wished that one day, all the birds would come back home to Zimbabwe. They never forgot how important these birds were. Did you know? Some of these birds were taken over 100 years ago! That's even longer than your grandparents have been alive! Imagine waiting that long for something so special to come back!

Coming Back Home!

Guess what? The wishes came true! People in Zimbabwe worked very hard and for a very long time to ask for their birds back. They spoke to people in different countries and explained how important these birds are to their culture and history. They never gave up! And slowly, one by one, the birds started to come back. It was like finding lost treasures! Each time a bird returned, there was so much joy and celebration. Everyone felt a little bit happier and more complete. The very last birds have just returned home! So now, *all* of them are back in Zimbabwe, safe and sound! Hooray! People are clapping, dancing, and cheering. It's a really happy celebration all over the country. What a wonderful feeling to have everything back where it belongs!

Why Are These Birds So Special?

These stone birds are not just pretty rocks. They are symbols. A symbol is like a picture or an object that stands for a big idea. These birds are symbols of Zimbabwe's strength, its rich history, and its amazing culture. They remind everyone of the smart and powerful kings and queens who lived there long ago. They help children learn about where they come from and how amazing and unique their country is. They show the world how rich and old African history truly is, filled with incredible stories and people. Did you know? The birds are a very important part of Zimbabwe's "national identity." This means they help make Zimbabwe, Zimbabwe! They are a special part of what makes the country unique and proud.

A Story for Everyone

This story is important for all of us, even if we don't live in Zimbabwe. It teaches us about being proud of who we are and where we come from. It teaches us about caring for our history, our special cultural things, and the stories of our ancestors. It also teaches us about working together, being determined, and never giving up to bring important things back home. The return of the birds shows that sometimes, even if things were wrong in the past, they can be made right again. It's a story of hope and determination for everyone.

A Happy Ending and a Bright Future!

Now that all the stone birds are home, where will they go? They will be kept safe and sound in special museums in Zimbabwe. Children and grown-ups from all over the country, and even from other parts of the world, can go to see them. They can learn about their history and feel connected to the past. They can feel proud that these wonderful birds, made by their clever ancestors, are finally back where they belong. This is a happy ending to a long story, but it's also a brand new beginning. The birds are home to tell their ancient stories to new generations of Zimbabwean children, who will grow up knowing their full history. What a wonderful day for Zimbabwe, and for all of Africa!
summary: All of Zimbabwe's very special and ancient stone bird statues, which were taken away long ago, have finally returned home to Zimbabwe, making everyone very happy.
whyMatters: These stone birds are super important because they show Zimbabwe's old history and culture, reminding everyone of their ancestors and making the country proud.
